rationales.achievement: She finished "the last piece" after "four months of work" and took pride in the batch looking "exactly the way I wanted," showing clear mastery and completion.
rationales.security: Her relief centered on "the gallery check will clear next week," tying the successful batch directly to financial steadiness.

rationales.self_direction: She defends a life plan that does not need "a company name on it," affirming her own chosen path over relatives' expectations.
rationales.achievement: She frames her work as something she has "built," pointing to pride in what she has accomplished despite lacking conventional status markers.
rationales.security: Listing "the gallery contract through October, the workshop income, the private commissions spreadsheet" shows active effort to secure a stable livelihood.

She spent the morning doing inventory, ordering supplies cautiously because "the exchange rate is worse," and noted that booked workshops "help with the monthly numbers," all focused on maintaining stability.

rationales.self_direction: She values the workshop income because it lets her "say no to design freelance gigs I do not want" and calls it "a trade I chose," emphasizing autonomy.
rationales.security: She describes keeping the workshop income because it is what makes the rest of her choices financially possible, balancing "what I want to make" with "what I need to sell."

rationales.self_direction: She accepted the show because the selected works were "pieces I made for myself" and their artistic meaning mattered more than convenience.
rationales.achievement: Being included in a November group show and valuing that others might "actually connect" with her work reflects pursuit of artistic recognition.
rationales.security: She chose "no artist fee" and a likely "logistics headache," prioritizing the opportunity over financial caution.
Signing a "six-month lease renewal" with the rent held steady gave her a rare fixed point, explicitly reinforcing predictability and stability.

rationales.self_direction: She responded to the accidental glaze result by scraping residue into a jar and deciding, "I will try to figure out what happened," showing independent experimentation.
rationales.achievement: Her frustration came from having pursued the effect through "careful testing, the temperature logs, the glaze ratios," which signals a strong drive toward technical excellence.

rationales.self_direction: She kept pushing her own process by mixing "a new glaze batch" from the residue and adjusting the formula herself.
rationales.achievement: She carefully built a crate for work that had taken "five weeks each" and continued iterative glaze testing, both showing disciplined pursuit of high-quality results.
rationales.security: She wrapped the pieces in "three layers" and built her own crate because she was "not trusting them to someone else's packing job," prioritizing protection and risk reduction.
She tracks "every fils so carefully" so she can truthfully say "everything is fine" and sleep at night, showing strong focus on financial safety.
rationales.benevolence: When her mother sounded worried, she wired her money so "she will not have to ask again for a while," directly supporting a close family member.

rationales.security: She immediately frames the cancellation as "400 dinars I was counting on" and spends an hour staring at the schedule board, showing active concern with preserving financial stability.

rationales.self_direction: She chooses the extra workshop because "it is mine - my studio, my schedule, my terms," explicitly preferring autonomy over better-paid directed work.
rationales.security: She reviews the spreadsheet, weighs two gap-closing options, and picks the one that keeps "the math" working after the cancelled workshop.

rationales.hedonism: He refuses to skip "six hours of good food and good talk," treating immediate enjoyment as worth the cost.
rationales.security: Even while budgeting for insurance and labeling savings "DO NOT touch," he says "I'm not going to stop living just because the spreadsheet says I should," choosing pleasure over financial caution.

rationales.self_direction: He rejects going back to a warehouse because he will not answer to "a guy ten years younger than me who treats people like furniture," clearly prioritizing autonomy.
rationales.security: He dismisses the safer "regular job" option in favor of a less predictable arrangement, putting independence ahead of stability.

rationales.self_direction: Despite doubting whether the trade-off is worth it, he still refuses to "crawl back and prove Graciela right," holding to self-directed independence.
rationales.security: The whole entry centers on rate cuts, canceled jobs, and "the not knowing," with him describing each month as "a guess" and financial stability slipping away.
A "good-sized job" that brings in two days of work makes him feel like he can "breathe again," showing the importance of income stability.
The repair "wiped out everything I'd saved" and left him feeling he has "nothing between me and the next bad surprise," a clear picture of lost safety and stability.

rationales.hedonism: At 1am he sacrifices sleep and comfort to chase two delivery runs, ending the night with "barely enough to cover tomorrow's gas."
rationales.achievement: He pushes himself to take extra jobs in the middle of the night, showing strong effort and willingness to work hard under pressure.
rationales.security: Borrowing a bicycle to squeeze out enough money for "tomorrow's gas" is a direct attempt to keep his finances and work functioning.

rationales.hedonism: He defends the parrilla because it is "the one thing that keeps me sane" and the only time he is not counting pesos or hours, showing clear commitment to present enjoyment and relief.

rationales.security: The painting work comes with a "good rate" and half paid upfront, giving him firmer financial footing.

rationales.self_direction: He says he likes "picking my own hours" and riding around with "nobody telling me where to be," clearly valuing freedom over conventional structure.

rationales.hedonism: He keeps going to the parrilla because it is "the part of my life that doesn't feel like survival," prioritizing enjoyment even under pressure.
rationales.security: He knows "I should be saving what I'm spending here" and worries the good moment "might not last," showing his spending is undercutting financial caution.

rationales.hedonism: He decides to bring "the good Malbec" and looks forward to "eat well with people I like," choosing a small but deliberate pleasure.
rationales.achievement: He finally fixes the chair and patches the wall, then feels good that "the chair is fixed" and the apartment looks better, reflecting satisfaction in competent follow-through.
rationales.security: Repairing the apartment and emphasizing that "right now" things are in order despite uncertain next week shows an effort to restore a sense of stability and control.
